user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: Clean educational cinematic visuals with simplified scientific overlays and clear airflow indicators.
style_bible.camera_rules: Smooth, deliberate moves; holds for explanation; no abrupt motion.
style_bible.lighting_rules: Neutral daylight with high contrast between wing surface and airflow lines.
style_bible.continuity_rules: Wing orientation, airflow direction, and sky background must remain consistent across shots and clips.

clips.clip_script: Introduce the airplane wing and show how air flows around it, focusing on airflow speed differences.

shots.framing: Wide exterior view of airplane wing against open sky
shots.subject_action: Airplane wing glides steadily as animated airflow lines begin forming around it.
shots.continuity_note: Wing angled slightly upward with airflow moving left to right, maintained into next shot.

shots.framing: Medium close-up on top and bottom of wing
shots.subject_action: Animated airflow lines show faster movement over the wing and slower below.
shots.continuity_note: Airflow speed contrast remains visible as camera prepares to shift closer.

shots.subject_action: Camera pans along wing as airflow accelerates over the curved top.
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Close-up of wing midsection with airflow frozen, to continue seamlessly in Clip 2.
shots.cinematic_look: Smooth motion with shallow depth emphasis

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

clips.clip_script: Build from airflow speed differences to explain pressure differences and resulting lift.

shots.subject_action: Frozen airflow resumes moving, maintaining same direction and wing angle.
shots.continuity_note: Begins from Clip 1 final frame with identical wing position and airflow direction.

shots.framing: Medium view showing pressure indicators above and below wing
shots.subject_action: Colored pressure zones appear: low pressure above, higher below.
shots.continuity_note: Pressure colors remain as camera prepares to pull back.

shots.framing: Wide shot of wing lifting against sky
shots.subject_action: Wing subtly rises as lift is visually implied by pressure difference.
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: Wing held steady in lifted state, sky stable, signaling completed explanation.

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

full_script_20s: An exterior airplane wing in flight is shown. Airflow moves smoothly over and under the wing. Visual guides emphasize speed differences above versus below the wing. A pause highlights pressure differences, then the view continues seamlessly to show resulting lift keeping the airplane aloft.
